segments.text: Some time later, the king’s cupbearer and baker offended their master, the king of Egypt.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cupbearer and the chief baker,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: and imprisoned them in the house of the captain of the guard, the same prison where Joseph was confined.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text:
The captain of the guard assigned them to Joseph, and he became their personal attendant.
After they had been in custody for some time,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: both of these men—the Egyptian king’s cupbearer and baker, who were being held in the prison—had a dream on the same night, and each dream had its own meaning.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: When Joseph came to them in the morning, he saw that they were distraught.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So he asked the officials of Pharaoh who were in custody with him in his master’s house,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Why are your faces so downcast today?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: We both had dreams,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied,
but there is no one to interpret them.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Then Joseph said to them,
Don’t interpretations belong to God? Tell me your dreams.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: So the chief cupbearer told Joseph his dream:
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: In my dream there was a vine before me,
segments.speaker: The chief cupbearer

segments.text: and on the vine were three branches. As it budded, its blossoms opened and its clusters ripened into grapes.
segments.speaker: The chief cupbearer

segments.text: Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes, squeezed them into his cup, and placed the cup in his hand.
segments.speaker: The chief cupbearer

segments.text: Joseph replied,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: This is the interpretation: The three branches are three days.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore your position. You will put Pharaoh’s cup in his hand, just as you did when you were his cupbearer.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: But when it goes well for you, please remember me and show me kindness by mentioning me to Pharaoh, that he might bring me out of this prison.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: For I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and even here I have done nothing for which they should have put me in this dungeon.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph,
segments.speaker: The chief baker

segments.text: I too had a dream: There were three baskets of white bread on my head.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: In the top basket were all sorts of baked goods for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.
segments.speaker: The chief baker

segments.text: Joseph replied,
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: This is the interpretation: The three baskets are three days.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: Within three days Pharaoh will lift off your head and hang you on a tree. Then the birds will eat the flesh of your body.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: On the third day, which was Pharaoh’s birthday, he held a feast for all his officials, and in their presence he lifted up the heads of the chief cupbearer and the chief baker.
Pharaoh restored the chief cupbearer to his position, so that he once again placed the cup in Pharaoh’s hand.
But Pharaoh hanged the chief baker, just as Joseph had described to them in his interpretation.
The chief cupbearer, however, did not remember Joseph; he forgot all about him.
